user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Evidence standard met. Requirement: The claimed object and relevant part should be visible clearly enough to inspect the claimed condition.
claim_status_justification: The submitted image (img_1) clearly shows the car's rear bumper with visible damage, satisfying the evidence standard for inspection. The observed condition is consistent with, and in fact more severe than, the claimed dent, supporting the reported rear bumper issue.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Evidence standard met. Requirement: The claimed object and relevant part should be visible clearly enough to inspect the claimed condition.
claim_status_justification: The front bumper is clearly visible in img_1 and shows damage consistent with a medium-severity impact, supporting the claim of front bumper damage. While the aggregated finding identifies a dent rather than a scratch, the affected part and condition are confirmed, and the claim is supported. Low light or glare conditions were noted but did not prevent adequate inspection of the damaged area.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. evidence_standard_met_reason: Evidence standard met. Requirement: The claimed object and relevant part should be visible clearly enough to inspect the claimed condition.
claim_status_justification: The windshield crack on the car is clearly visible in img_1 and img_2, meeting the evidence standard for inspection. No contradictions were identified, and the medium severity is consistent with a typical crack claim. The claim is supported.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.

The evidence standard is met, and the aggregated assessment of glass_shatter at medium severity on the side_mirror aligns with the claimant's description. user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Evidence standard met. Requirement: The claimed object and relevant part should be visible clearly enough to inspect the claimed condition.
claim_status_justification: The claim is contradicted because img_2 clearly shows the rear bumper with only minor, non-impact damage, failing to substantiate the described dent. The visual evidence does not match the severity of the reported condition, and this is compounded by a history of exaggerated vehicle damage claims, warranting manual review.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted images do not clearly show the claimed object or part.
claim_status_justification: The submitted images do not clearly show the claimed headlight or the described crack, so the evidence standard is not met. Additionally, the claim indicates severe damage to the headlight while the available evidence shows only minor or unrelated issues, creating a mismatch between the reported and observed condition. Without clearer visual confirmation of the cracked headlight, there is not enough information to adjudicate this claim.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.

claim_status_justification: The laptop hinge shows a visible crack in img_1, consistent with the claimed broken part condition and meeting the evidence standard. Although the image has some low light/glare, the hinge damage is clearly inspectable and corroborates the prior accepted hinge claim.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.

Risk flags including blurry images, low light/glare, and damage not being visible further confirm that insufficient visual evidence exists to adjudicate this claim.

The evidence standard is met with the relevant part visible enough to inspect the condition, and no contradictions were identified. user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
